div1.entryFree: Outwear, to pass, to spend, to waste (used only of time): her song was tedious and outwore the night, Ven. 841. shows me a bare-boned death by time outworn, Lucr. 1761. the rich proud cost of outworn buried age, Sonn. 64, 2. thus is his cheek the map of days outworn, 68, 1 (== times past). till painful study shall o. three years, LLL II, 23. the sun is high, and we o. the day, H5 IV, 2, 63.
